# Updated: 2026-05-13 by CLAUDE_CODE (Opus 4.7 direct execution — Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-4 Notifications + Alert History Export squash-merged to main (commit ab5a782). Final-ship push to origin/main pending.)

PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-4 — Notifications + Alert History Export squash-merged to main (commit ab5a782). Two new per-entity export endpoints live on main: /api/exports/notifications (tenant+user-scoped, isRead + notificationType filters, 9-column NotificationRow with flattened relation columns) and /api/exports/alert-history (tenant-scoped, alertRuleId filter, 8-column AlertHistoryRow with both immutable snapshot fields and current relation values falling back to "(deleted)"). After SS-4 final-ship push, Item 4 (PDF/CSV Exports) is COMPLETE — all 5 entity export endpoints live (events, patrols, alert-rules, notifications, alert-history). Only Real-time SSE remains in Phase 8 Batch 2 backlog. Prior shipped this session: SS-3 Alert Rules Export (commit 1e03188 + 5252df2 + 0070eeb), SS-2 Patrols Export (52869ae + 72adb33 + e735686), SS-1 Events Export (0171309 + 3ed3382 + 1c783cd), SS-0 Export Foundation (4caa449).

LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-4 — Notifications + Alert History Export feature work squash-merged to main (commit ab5a782). Following the SS-1/SS-2/SS-3 cadence ×2 in one branch: TWO routes shipped together since both are audit-style views with similar column shapes.

              Files added:
              - `apps/web/src/app/api/exports/notifications/route.ts`: GET Route Handler with `// Non-tRPC: manual auth required` header (security.md L11). Flow: requireRouteAuth() → rateLimiters.upload.check(userId) (20/min) → coerce query-string `isRead` ("true"/"false"/undefined) → boolean; pass through `notificationType` as raw string to Zod enum validator → notificationListFilters.safeParse → prisma.tenant.findUniqueOrThrow for slug+name → prisma.notification.findMany with `where: {tenantId, userId, ...isRead, ...notificationType}` (note USER scoping — notifications are per-user) + `include: {alertRule, event, patrol}` for relation flattening + `take: 10001` for overflow detection → HTTP 413 JSON if length > 10000 → flatten to typed NotificationRow (9 columns: id, title, message, type=notificationType, isRead="true"/"false", alertRuleName=alertRule?.name??"", eventTitle=event?.title??"", patrolTitle=patrol?.title??"", createdAt ISO) → sha256 filterHash → CSV/PDF branches via toCsv()/renderExportPdf() → writeExportAudit() AFTER success → NextResponse with Content-Type/Disposition/Cache-Control: no-store. Relation columns use nullish coalescing to "" since alertRule/event/patrol are all optional FKs on the Notification model.
              - `apps/web/src/app/api/exports/notifications/__tests__/route.test.ts`: 6 tests — CSV byte-level BOM (carrying SS-1 🟡 fix) + tenant+user-scoped findMany (where: {tenantId: "t1", userId: "u1"}) + flattened relation columns body assertion (CSV contains "Whale Sighting" event title + "Priority 200+" alert rule name); PDF content-type; 401 missing session + findMany not called; 413 when findMany returns 10001 rows + no audit row written; DATA_EXPORT audit shape with entityType="notifications" + 64-char-hex filterHash + changesJson={format,rowCount}; isRead+notificationType filter propagation into prisma.where with boolean coercion verified.
              - `apps/web/src/app/api/exports/alert-history/route.ts`: GET Route Handler same shape as notifications. Flow: requireRouteAuth() → rateLimiters.upload.check(userId) → alertHistoryListFilters.safeParse({alertRuleId}) → prisma.tenant.findUniqueOrThrow → prisma.alertHistory.findMany with `where: {tenantId, ...alertRuleId}` + `include: {alertRule, event}` + `take: 10001` + `orderBy: {firedAt: "desc"}` → HTTP 413 on overflow → flatten to typed AlertHistoryRow (8 columns: id, ruleNameSnapshot, eventTitleSnapshot, matchedPriority as string, recipientCount as string, alertRuleCurrent=alertRule?.name??"(deleted)", eventCurrent=event?.title??"(deleted)", firedAt ISO) — the (deleted) fallback honors the SetNull onDelete behavior of the alert_history schema, exposing BOTH snapshot AND current views since alertHistory rows survive deletion of their FK targets → sha256 filterHash → CSV/PDF branches → writeExportAudit() with entityType="alert-history" → NextResponse.
              - `apps/web/src/app/api/exports/alert-history/__tests__/route.test.ts`: 6 tests — CSV BOM + tenant-scoped findMany + snapshot column body assertions (CSV contains "Priority 200+" rule snapshot + "Whale Sighting" event snapshot + "220" matched priority); PDF content-type; 401; 413 + no audit row; DATA_EXPORT audit shape with entityType="alert-history"; alertRuleId filter propagation into prisma.where.

              Files modified:
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/notification.ts`: extracted `notificationListFilters = z.object({ isRead: z.boolean().optional(), notificationType: z.enum(["critical","warning","info","system"]).optional() })` as a new top-level export. notificationRouter.list now uses `notificationListFilters.extend({cursor, limit})` for its input schema. Mirrors the SS-1 eventListFilters / SS-2 patrolListFilters / SS-3 alertRuleListFilters extracts — single source of truth for filter validation, consumed by both tRPC list and the Route Handler.
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/alertHistory.ts`: extracted `alertHistoryListFilters = z.object({ alertRuleId: z.string().optional() })` as a new top-level export. alertHistoryRouter.list now uses `alertHistoryListFilters.extend({cursor, limit})` for its input schema.
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/notifications/page.tsx`: added buildExportUrl import; inserted 2 Export buttons inside the existing `<div className="flex items-center gap-3">` header right-side group, positioned BEFORE the existing Select + Mark all as read. Export buttons pass `{notificationType: typeFilter==="all"?undefined:typeFilter}` so the exported CSV/PDF matches the user's current filtered view.
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/alerts/history/page.tsx`: added buildExportUrl import; wrapped the existing "Back to Alert Rules" Button in a new `<div className="flex items-center gap-2">` group and prepended 2 Export buttons. No filter passed since the page does not yet expose alertRuleId filter UI — the export defaults to all alert history for the tenant.
              - `docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md`: new SS-4 entry prepended above SS-3.
              - `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md`: Last updated line refreshed; SS-4 item flipped from `[ ]` to `[x]` with full details; Next Step section rewritten — Item 4 functionally complete, only Real-time SSE remains in Phase 8 Batch 2; Shipped since list gains SS-3 final-ship marker + SS-4 entry.

              Schema/migrations: none — SS-4 is application code only.

              - lint 5/5 packages clean (zero fixups — SS-3's lint patterns for required-FK relation includes carried forward verbatim)
              - tests: 84/84 in apps/web (was 72 — added 6 notifications + 6 alert-history route handler tests) + 7/7 in packages/jobs (cached, unchanged) = 91 total

              EXECUTION NOTES:
              Per the documented hook-injection thrashing lesson, stayed Opus-direct throughout. Hook injection still firing this session (vercel-plugin "vercel-functions"/"next-cache-components"/"nextjs"/"next-forge" suggestions on Read+Write+Edit; context-mode observation pointers on every Read; claude-mem timeline pointers; PostToolUse-validate suggestions on both new route files for "observability instrumentation" — rejected, matches SS-1/SS-2/SS-3 instrumentation surface i.e. none). Opus 4.7 absorbed all of them cleanly. Tier 2 estimate held — both routes + tests + UI + governance in one session under the 22K target without thrashing.

              Plan deviations: none of substance. Notifications row format chose 9 columns matching SS-3's lean column count. AlertHistory row format chose 8 columns and intentionally surfaces BOTH snapshot fields (ruleNameSnapshot, eventTitleSnapshot — immutable values captured at fire time) AND current relation values (alertRuleCurrent, eventCurrent, falling back to "(deleted)" via nullish coalescing) — this is the honest representation because alertHistory rows survive the deletion of their alertRule/event via SetNull onDelete, and consumers of the export need both perspectives.
